---
title: "Traits and AquaX Sample - cont."
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
    message: false
    warning: false
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(terra)
```

```{r}
# Not on server
load('data/META_02122025.Rdata')
# Load functional entitity data from O'hara et al. 2024
fe_spp <- read_csv('data/2024_ohara_fe_species.csv')
# Load sample data
aquax_sample <- read_csv('data/2026_OHARA_TST_MORPHO/sample_for_aquaX.csv') 
```

```{r}
META_clean <- META %>% 
  dplyr::select(SPID, GROUP, AphiaID, ScientificName) %>% 
  mutate(species = tolower(ScientificName))

# 101,361 spp of 206,864 have quick matches (still get 376 of 373 fe)
xwlk <- inner_join(META_clean, fe_spp)
```

```{r}
# All spp have fe matchs --> 163 fe
aquax_sample_xwlk <- inner_join(aquax_sample, fe_spp) %>% 
  rename(aphiaid = AphiaID)
```

## Build species information

Associate SDM files with the metadata for the 564 sampled species
```{r}
spp_fs <- list.files('/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/2026_OHARA_TST_MORPHO/SDM',
                     full.names = TRUE)

df_spp <- data.frame(f = spp_fs) %>%
    mutate(fsize = file.size(f),
           aphiaid = str_extract(basename(f), '[0-9]+') %>% as.integer()) %>%
    # Join in the trait crosswalk
    left_join(aquax_sample_xwlk)
```

## Work on applying to full sample

```{r}
xwlk_simple <- aquax_sample_xwlk %>% 
  dplyr::select(aphiaid,fe_id)
```

```{r}
#set.seed(123)
#test_spp <- slice_sample(df, n = 30)
```

```{r}
trait_change <- function(id, df) {
  spp_filter <- df %>% 
    filter(aphiaid == id) 
  
  load(spp_filter$f)
  
  spp_change <- FINALEMMEAN %>% 
  mutate(aphiaid = id) %>% 
  select(x, y, aphiaid, Current, RCP85_2050, cutoff) %>% 
  mutate(historical = case_when(Current < cutoff ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1),
         future = case_when(RCP85_2050 < cutoff ~ 0,
                             TRUE ~ 1)) #%>%
  # historical column plus 2 * future gives 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence
  #mutate(pxl_change = historical + 2 * future)
  
  spp_change_fe_id <- inner_join(spp_change, xwlk_simple)
  
  r_spp_change <- rast(spp_change_fe_id)
  
  # return all fields
  return(wrap(r_spp_change))
}
```

## Loop through the functional entity codes, apply function, mosaic into a single layer for each FE, and calculate the pixel change by trait.
Can still parallelize the function within the loop

```{r}
# Identify fe_id groups in sample
fe_ids <- unique(df_spp$fe_id)

# Create empty list for results 
all_results <- list()
```

```{r}
# Took 50 minutes using 10 cores
system.time({
for (id in fe_ids) {
  # Filter for functional entity 
  df_sub <- df_spp %>% filter(fe_id == id)
  
  aphiaid_sub <- unique(df_sub$aphiaid)
  
  # Now iterate over the aphiaids (species) using trait_change function
    wrapped_r <- parallel::mclapply(aphiaid_sub, FUN = trait_change, df = df_sub, 
                                    mc.cores = 10)
    
    # Unwrap rasters (they're wrapped for parallelization)
    spp_r <- lapply(wrapped_r, unwrap)
    
    # Create sprc and mosaic to create one raster layer of the trait
    change_collection <- sprc(spp_r)
    # Using "max" to assign the pixel 1 if any pixel in stack has 1
    change_stack_max <- mosaic(change_collection, fun = "max")
    # Get trait change at pixel level
    # historical + 2 * future gives 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence
    change_stack_trait_change <- change_stack_max[["historical"]] + 2 * change_stack_max[["future"]]
    # Change name
    names(change_stack_trait_change) <- as.character(id)
    
    # Return results
    all_results[[as.character(id)]] <- change_stack_trait_change
}
})
```

```{r}
#all_results_137 <- all_results[[2]]
```

```{r}
#plot(all_results_137)
```

## Map out trait change

```{r}
# Align extent of the rasters
all_results_collection <- sprc(all_results)
max_extent <- ext(all_results_collection )
```

```{r}
align_list <- function(r) {
  extend(r, max_extent)
}
```

```{r}
trait_rast_aligned <- lapply(all_results, align_list)
```

```{r}
trait_stack <- rast(trait_rast_aligned)
```

## Now need to count up the numbers 
The count of 0s, 1s, 2s, and 3s can be exploiting to figure out historic and future presence of the trait: 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence

```{r}
count_change <- function(x, n){
  sum(x == n, na.rm = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
# Took 3 min
system.time({
  trait_same_count_pl <- app(trait_stack, count_change, n = 3, cores = 10)
  })
```

```{r}
# Took 3 min
system.time({
  trait_inv_count_pl <- app(trait_stack, count_change, n = 2, cores = 10)
  })
```

```{r}
# Took 3 min
system.time({
  trait_ext_count_pl <- app(trait_stack, count_change, n = 1, cores = 10)
  })
```

```{r}
trait_ssi_pl <- (2*trait_same_count_pl)/((2*trait_same_count_pl) + trait_ext_count_pl + trait_inv_count_pl)
```

```{r}
plot(trait_ssi_pl)
```
```{r}
writeRaster(trait_stack, 
            filename = "/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/outputs/trait_stack_sample_031826.tif")
```


```{r}
writeRaster(trait_ext_count_pl, 
            filename = "/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/outputs/trait_ext_031826.tif")
writeRaster(trait_inv_count_pl, 
            filename = "/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/outputs/trait_inv_031826.tif")
writeRaster(trait_same_count_pl, 
            filename = "/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/outputs/trait_same_031826.tif")
```

```{r}
writeRaster(trait_ssi_pl, 
            filename = "/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/outputs/trait_ssi_031826.tif")
```


```{r}
trait_ssi_df <- as.data.frame(trait_ssi_pl, xy = TRUE)
trait_h_df <- as.data.frame(trait_ext_count_pl + trait_same_count_pl, xy = TRUE)
trait_f_df <- as.data.frame(trait_inv_count_pl + trait_same_count_pl, xy = TRUE)
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = trait_h_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = lyr.1)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(0,100), direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "AquaX Subsample: Historic Functional Entity Count", 
       fill = "FE count")
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = trait_f_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = lyr.1)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(0,100), direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "AquaX Subsample: Future Functional Entity Count", 
       fill = "FE count")
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_raster(data = trait_ssi_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = lyr.1)) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(0,1), direction = -1) +
  labs(title = "AquaX Subsample: 153 trait SSI (564 species)", 
       fill = "SSI")
```


###### Old code

## Inspect a test species

Identify the structure of the SDM .Rdata for a single species

```{r}
df_5 <- df %>% 
  # Filter for one fe_id
  filter(fe_id ==5)
```

```{r}
extract_change <- function(id, df) {
  spp_filter <- df %>% 
    filter(aphiaid == id) 
  
  load(spp_filter$f)
  
  spp_change <- FINALEMMEAN %>% 
  mutate(aphiaid = id) %>% 
  select(x, y, aphiaid, Current, RCP85_2050, cutoff) %>% 
  mutate(historical = case_when(Current < cutoff ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1),
         future = case_when(RCP85_2050 < cutoff ~ 0,
                             TRUE ~ 1)) %>%
  # historical column plus 2 * future gives 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence
  mutate(pxl_change = historical + 2 * future)
  
  r_spp_change <- rast(spp_change)
  
  # return just the pixel change field
  #r_spp_single <- r_spp_change[[col]]
  #return(wrap(r_spp_single))
  
  # return all fields
  return(wrap(r_spp_change))
}
```

```{r}
aphiaid_df_5 <- df_5$aphiaid
```

```{r}
# Took 5 mins.
system.time({
  out_list_r_pll <- parallel::mclapply(aphiaid_df_5, FUN = extract_change, df = df_5,
                                       #col = "historical",
                                       mc.cores = 1)
})
```

```{r}
# Unwrap rasters (30 seconds)
system.time({
  final_rasters_pll <- lapply(out_list_r_pll, unwrap)
  })
```

```{r}
# The rasters are not the same extent (different cropping based on species), so I need to union them to find the largest area and then pad the individual rasters
# LAA - could we just use extent of globe for the full analysis and skip these steps? (these steps take a handful of minutes)
change_collection <- sprc(final_rasters_pll)
max_extent <- ext(change_collection)
```

```{r}
align_list <- function(r) {
  extend(r, max_extent)
}
```

```{r}
change_rast_aligned <- lapply(final_rasters_pll, align_list)
```

```{r}
change_stack <- rast(change_rast_aligned)
```

```{r}
# Mosaic uses spcc
# For each pixel, if there is at least one species present with that trait (1), then a 1 will be captured.
change_stack_max <- mosaic(change_collection, fun = "max")
```

```{r}
change_stack_max_change <- change_stack_max[["historical"]] + 2 * change_stack_max[["future"]]
names(change_stack_max_change) <- "trait"
```

```{r}
# Plottign fe_id == 5 
plot(change_stack_max[["historical"]])
plot(change_stack_max[["future"]])
plot(change_stack_max[["historical"]] + 2 * change_stack_max[["future"]])
```
